title: Latent-KV Attention Audit Remaining Queue
research_question: If all-layer latent-KV loses, is the real issue full-stack placement rather than the latent-KV mechanism itself?

candidates.name: i3-upper-latent64
candidates.hypothesis: Upper layers may tolerate K/V compression better than earlier layers, so localizing the latent bottleneck to the top of the stack could recover much of the all-layer quality loss.
candidates.description: upper-layer latent-KV placement test against the all-layer latent64 baseline

candidates.name: i4-lower-latent64
candidates.hypothesis: Early layers may be the cleaner place to compress K/V while preserving later full-fidelity attention, so lower-only latent-KV could beat all-layer compression.
candidates.description: lower-layer latent-KV placement test against the all-layer latent64 baseline

candidates.name: i5-10l-all-latent64
candidates.hypothesis: The stronger latent-KV line may only make sense if its saved memory is reinvested into one more layer, so 10L plus all-layer latent64 could outperform the no-reinvestment version.
candidates.description: reinvest all-layer latent64 savings into a tenth layer
